title: LIMIT clause on sub-select in FROM clause of a SELECT in a UNION ALL interpreted incorrectly
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE test_table (id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY, name TEXT, value REAL, created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP);
INSERT INTO test_table (name, value) VALUES ('Bob', NULL);
SELECT * FROM (SELECT id, name FROM test_table WHERE value > 5 LIMIT 1) AS subquery1 UNION ALL SELECT * FROM (SELECT id, name FROM test_table WHERE value IS NULL LIMIT 1) AS subquery2;

SELECT COUNT(*) FROM (SELECT id FROM test_table WHERE created_at < '2023-01-01' LIMIT 2) AS subquery1 UNION ALL SELECT COUNT(*) FROM (SELECT id FROM test_table WHERE name LIKE 'A%' LIMIT 3) AS subquery2;
key_bug_features: LIMIT clause on sub-select in UNION ALL
